title: CWS inbound WS frame
description: Top-level cws-comm WebSocket frame as received by the SDK. The transport layer (transport/ws.js) parses raw text into this JSON object and hands it to createFrameDispatcher (protocol/frame-dispatch.js). Frames are permissive by design: a frame with an unrecognized `type` classifies as FrameKind `unknown` and is dropped, so this schema intentionally allows additional top-level and payload properties. `payload` for a `message` frame may either carry the message notification fields directly or nest them one level deeper under `payload.payload` (the orchestrator reads `payload?.payload || payload`).

type.description: Wire-level frame type. Drives classifyFrame() -> FrameKind.

payload.description: Type-specific body. Shape depends on `type`; see the $defs payload shapes.

FrameKind.title: FrameKind
FrameKind.description: Classification bucket returned by classifyFrame() (protocol/frame-dispatch.js FRAME_KIND). This is the SDK-neutral kind, NOT the wire `type`.

FrameType.title: FrameType
FrameType.description: Known wire `type` values. `message`->message, `message_ack`->message_ack, `system`->system, `error`->error, `ping`/`pong`->heartbeat, `typing`/`presence`/`read_receipt`/`read_state_update`->presence. Any other string is allowed on the wire and classifies as `unknown`.

MessageNotification.title: MessageNotification
MessageNotification.description: The `payload` of a `message` frame (or the object nested under payload.payload for /sync catch-up envelopes). Only id + conversation_id are required for the orchestrator to proceed; the rest are hoisted/merged with the fetched message detail. `_via` is an SDK-internal arrival-path marker ('sync' for /sync catch-up).

MessageAckPayload.title: MessageAckPayload
MessageAckPayload.description: The `payload` of a `message_ack` frame (server ack of an outbound send).

SystemPayload.title: SystemPayload
SystemPayload.description: The `payload` of a `system` frame. `event` is a cws-comm domain event name (classifySystemEvent maps it to recall/edit/config_update/connection/channel/null). `data` carries event-specific fields (message_id/id/msg_id, recalled_by/edited_by/sender_id, conversation_id, agent_member_id, sender_type).
